hawkish_phrases: measures of underlying inflation remained subdued and had yet to show convincing signs of a sustained upward trend
evidence remained insufficient at the current stage
wages continued to edge higher and were expected to lend support to price pressures
emerging signs of labour shortages and capacity constraints
inflation convergence would likely proceed only gradually
dovish_phrases: incoming information had generally surprised to the downside and pointed to some moderation
downside risks related to the threat of trade protectionism having become more prominent
a more pronounced weakening of demand, notably related to external factors, could therefore not be ruled out
ample degree of monetary policy accommodation remained necessary
patience, persistence and prudence with regard to monetary policy remained warranted
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged at 0.00% (main refinancing), 0.25% (marginal lending), and -0.40% (deposit) with unanimous decision, maintaining €30 billion monthly asset purchases through at least September 2018, with a mildly dovish bias reflecting increased uncertainty from trade tensions and moderation in growth despite maintaining confidence in medium-term inflation convergence.
